text:
Team GB silver medalist Matthew Hudson-Smithhas admitted he has been unable to reply to messages from Noah Lyles sincehis 400 metre heartbreak on Wednesday night. The 29-year-old clocked a new European record of 43.44 seconds only to be denied on the line by American Quincy Hall, who ran his own lifetime best of 43.40 seconds.

It meant another agonising second place on the world stage for Hudson-Smith, who missed gold by just 0.09 seconds at the World Athletics Championships 12 months ago.

And he also failed to match his former housemate Lyles,after the American landed 100m gold on Sunday.

Speaking toEurosporton Thursday morning, Hudson-Smith was asked if he had received any messages from Lyles, who is still aiming for a sprint double having reached the 200m final. “He messaged me beforehand and it was, well, I didn’t read it because I was just so focussed on like the task at hand,” he admitted.

Pressed on whether he’d since read the message and replied, Hudson-Smith claimed: “I still haven’t had the chance to read it.” One presenter then joked: “You ghosted Noah Lyles? That’s pretty big time.”

Hudson-Smith did then see the funny side of the conversation, when he was asked about Lyles’ previous claims about the bad habits of the Team GB athlete when they lived together in the US. “Why’s he baiting me out man?” he asked, while laughing.



“I could bait him out so much with that. But he’s a global superstar now guys, so I’ve got to leave some mystery out there.” He also vowed to go and visit the 27-year-old once he left theBBCstudio.

Hudson-Smith did cut a far more jovial figure than the one of the previous evening, having fallen short of becoming Team GB’s first maleOlympic400m champion since Eric Liddell 100 years ago. But immediately after his race, he vowed to go for gold again in Los Angeles in four years’ time.

And he even outlined his ambition to challenge the world record time of 43.03 seconds, set by South Africa’s Wayde van Niekerk in the Olympic final in Rio in 2016. “I’m the best in the world when I’m healthy,” said Hudson-Smith.

“This is just the start. I’ll lower the time in the 400m and go for a 19 seconds in the 200m and shake things up a bit more. When it clicks, it is going to be special. I was in the world-record race, so I know what is like to be in a calibre race where everyone is crazy and talented.”

The Games are not yet done for Hudson-Smith. He’ll now be part of the Team GB men’s 4x400m relay team, with their first heat scheduled for Friday morning.

title: Heartbroken Team GB star 'ghosted' 100m Olympic champion Noah Lyles | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson
description: Matthew Hudson-Smith missed out on 400m gold in agonising fashion on Wednesday night.
